On the day of my hearing the most important date, Mr Good senior sends his son to represent me, his son makes me sign a new client form to allow him to represent me. Of course I sign with his son out of desperation because my hearing is in 10 minutes. His son Good jr acted very crass towards the judge and ultimately ended up threatening the judge by saying I don't care what decision you make we will appeal! I signed up with Mr Good senior, someone I thought was an honest experienced attorney and he completely let me down and sent his inexperienced son to represent me at my hearing last minute! Again go with the bigger groups you see on TV... this law group is no good!

benchmark_result.review_text: If you want to pay someone $3,500 just to be screamed at and thrown out of their office, then this is the lawyer for you. I hired him to handle my case, but during what should have been a routine office visit, he lost his temper simply because I was wearing a beer T-shirt under a zipped hoodie. When I calmly told him I didn’t appreciate being spoken to that way—especially after paying him thousands of dollars—he threw my folder across the desk, told me it was “16 hours of work” and that I wouldn’t be getting anything back, then ordered his secretary to kick me out. I never received a refund, never got proper legal help from him, and ultimately had to hire another attorney (Albert Altamore) to fix the mess. This incident happened about a year ago, and I waited until all my court matters were resolved before posting this review to make sure he couldn’t jeopardize my case. I’m writing this now to warn others: save your money, time, and peace of mind.

benchmark_result.review_text: If I could give 6 stars, I would. I made a terrible mistake and was charged with a DUI. I’ve never been in trouble with the law, not even a ticket, so my anxiety was eating me alive. I came across Paul and, after reading all of the reviews, I chose him to represent me in court, and it was probably one of the best decisions I made. Not only did he reassure me when it came to anxiety, but he was hands-on every step of the way. Every time I had a question or concern about anything, he responded quickly, whether it was through text or call. He fought for me until the very end and got me the best possible outcome. He was able to get my license suspension lifted for me with having to take a few treatment classes, court supervision, and pay fines, so if you’re ever in need of an attorney, whatever the case may be, Paul is your guy to go to. Thank you again, Paul, you’re the best!

Almost feel like this gentleman just enjoyed hearing his own voice. Will never attempt to go through this lawfirm again and would not recommend anybody else to try them. The biggest kick is that this gentleman casually threw in my face that the amount of money I was seeking legal action for was basically chump change and not worth his time. Well not everybody makes big lawyer money like you my friend, for some people that amount of money could genuinely be life changing.

benchmark_result.review_text: I have great reservations about not awarding the firm five stars. Thus far, the representation has been outstanding. The lawyers are caring, intelligent, know the law, and are aggressive in representing my interests, and I am sure, the interests of all there clients. That the litigation is more is expensive than I, and I am sure they, anticipated is because the circumstances so unusual that they aren't likely to happen again anytime soon and it would have happened whatever law firm I retained. That said, trying to deal with the billing department is a nightmare. That is not to say that any of the enumerated costs in the billing statements I have received are out of the ordinary--if anything certain things appear to be billed somewhat conservatively: in my experience, O'Flaherty law compares favorably with the standard rates for much smaller firms that do not have nearly the same resources and experience. However, dealing with the billing department is a nightmare. Once, I thought a retainer had been paid, and months later I was notified it was not and that representation would be put on hold. Sometimes, a retainer refill will be $5,000, sometimes $10,000, and the discrepancy is never explained. Very shortly after paying the 10,000 I got a letter saying "your retainer has been depleted and is insufficient to cover a past-due balance," and asked for $5,0000. I was shocked, asked about it, and was told that it had not been depleted, that the 5,000 was essentially to get the trust back up to 10,000 but that the matter had been placed on hold until this was paid. Yet the due date to pay it was seven days later making me wonder if the matter had been placed on hold or would be placed on hold in seven days. (Aren't lawyers supposed to be precise about these things?) Nor does it seem had my lawyer paid any heed (he was more worried about my case than the billing department and there was still money in the trust.) and a couple of weeks later while considering how I would pay the retainer, I got a past due balance for a much smaller amount (under 2,000 dollars), but nowhere did it say the matter would be placed on hold until the outstanding balance was paid which perhaps would have been the most sensible place to take such action. Aren't lawyers supposed to be precise with language? Again, as far as I can tell there is nothing enumerated in the invoices that I can see which isn't relevant, necessary, or prudent in regards to the litigation, and when enumerated is billed at a very reasonable rate for a lawyer. The problem is that what the billing department says doesn't make any sense and when you ask for clarification, they respond with "oh what it says isn't true." Also, the enumerated hours come in the mail, and unsurprisingly will come at completely different time then email requests for a retainer to be refilled or a past due balance to be paid. It might be helpful it there were a secure online portal where one could examine the amount paid, the expenses billed, the retainer, any unpaid balances. I also asked the billing department how much the case had cost in total, but I didn't receive a response. (I've asked again, and I'm sure I will.) This is not over-billing or anything sinister, but it is a mess.

benchmark_result.review_text: I had a consultation with Mr. Oancea regarding a business Small Claims Court case in Cook County, and I’m very grateful for his guidance. He carefully reviewed our case, explained the process step by step, and clearly outlined what we could realistically expect. What stood out most was his honesty — he did not make unrealistic promises, but instead gave straightforward, practical advice based on experience. I truly felt that he cared and wanted to help. I highly recommend Iurie Oancea as an attorney in Cook County. Thank you for your time and professionalism. I truly appreciate your honesty, guidance, and support.

benchmark_result.review_text: Where do I start with this review. We were referred to McNamee & Maheney Ltd. by a close friend and contacted them right away, gave our deposit and we never heard from them for over a year. We tried and tried to contact the office and always got told that our message was left for our lawyer, come to find out he did not even work there anymore and our case was forgotten about. We tried for another couple months to get answers and got nothing. We finally got an appointment, when we showed up they said the lawyer was on vacation and we still could not get our deposit back, and they would send a check in the mail. Two weeks went by and I called to see if it was in the mail, and they had not sent it and would put in directly in our account, finally three days later it was there. So bottom line if you want to get ignored and forgot about go with McNamee & Mahoney, but save your money and find another lawyer!

benchmark_result.review_text: I hired Dane Loizzo as my third attorney during my divorce case. Within just three weeks, Dane racked up over $7,000 in charges, many of which appeared inflated, before withdrawing from my case without achieving any meaningful progress. Dane’s father, Tom Loizzo, is a well-regarded attorney with a strong reputation, and my mother has worked with Tom for decades. However, my experience with Dane was entirely different. He failed to provide value, transparency, or support. Based on my experience, I would not recommend Dane Loizzo for any divorce-related matters.

benchmark_result.review_text: 4.5 Star Rating! The stars above did not give me that option. The short story is I hired Attorney Mehlos to help me fight a battery charge. The real story is Corey helped me through the worst year of my life. The short: I was being attacked and called the police. When they arrived I did not want the "attacker" arrested, because it was really just an argument that went to far and I was not injured. That being the case I did not say anything other than "Thanks for coming. Everything is okay". Unfortunately the other party was very mad and completely lied to the police and I was arrested. I was innocent and it was frustrating because they also too things that were said completely out of context. From day 1, Attorney Mehlos and I came up with a plan and continued to refine our strategy until the prosecutor dismissed the battery charge, before we were to go to trial. It took 12 months to get it done and it nearly killed me. Obviously there is a lot more to it, but I am not writting a book! LOL There was a lot of back and forth with the Prosecutor Assistant D.A. and the Judges. It was completely overwhelming at times to be an innocent man who has never been convicted of any crime and yet it seemed that everyone except Attorney Mehlos assumed I was guilty. It was comforting to know that Attorney Mehlos was confident and prepared to win at trial. The prosecutor eventually released knowing he’d have a tough case. Yet on multiple occasions Attorney Mehlos told me to trust him and the result was exactly what he predicted. I highly recommend Attorney Mehlos for any criminal case. Sincerely, Jeff R. Brownell Owner & Operator TaskMasters Home Services LLC

benchmark_result.review_text: In January of 2023, after a 26 year untarnished and successful career as a firefighter/paramedic and 12 years as an adjunct fire instructor, I was falsely accused, charged and arrested for 5 different counts which consisted of both felonies and misdemeanors ranging from 4th degree sexual assault of a child to misconduct in office. During the 5 days I spent in jail, my wife received a recommendation to call attorney Corey Mehlos. That decision turned out to be the one that saved my life both literally and figuratively. The passion, professionalism, determination, dedication and commitment not just from Corey, but his entire staff is second to none. He never gave up on me or my family and after nearly 3 long years, his tireless work ended with an absolute domination in the court room. I was found not guilty and cleared overwhelmingly on all charges. This man is the absolute definition of what a defense attorney should be. He is truly fearless, and I wouldn’t hesitate to recommend him to anyone and every one who finds themself in need of legal assistance. Casper Mehlos law firm is THE REAL DEAL

we spent around 15-20 grand
never really did anything towards my son's appeal

benchmark_result.review_text:
I truly wonder if other Black people had great experiences here. They accepted thousands of dollars but never really did anything towards my son's appeal, never really fought. In the beginning, one of the assistant attorneys (maybe even a paralegal, not too sure) that worked the case seemed to be good for a quick sec, but then was transferred or something. The case was transferred to one of the law firm attorneys. I believed he emailed me once, never really talked to me or updated me much. Then the case was given to a younger lady whom handled it until the decision. They never even really had meetings with me or my son. Truly horrible, and adds to how certain persons are treated in this business.

I read the other reviews on how this firm worked with those persons, my family and our case received none of this. I don't even think I ever met with anyone other than the paralegal but we spent around 15-20 grand. Truly a disappointing experience.

benchmark_result.review_text: I’m disappointed in the service provided by Fields Law firm. My son’s appeal was denied and I understand that is how insurance companies work but it was sent directly to the disability provider without our being able to review. Follow-up discussion included a recommendation to go back to settle and waive benefits for life. However, most recently I sent them an email and no response. Then out of the blue they send me an email to which I replied and no response. I’m left wondering if the attorney on our case is even still employed there.

benchmark_result.review_text:
I wish I could write a positive review, but unfortunately, I cannot. As I told my lawyer several times, I was not happy with the outcome. I feel my lawyer was representing the defendant instead of me. It seemed that my lawyer was not trying to get me to the best outcome. I spent hours building spreadsheets explaining the way the defendant cost me lifetime money, but my lawyer seemed to ignore my concerns. It was my lawyer who talked me out of going to court before a judge. The final decision was mine based on my lawyer telling to accept the "final offer".

I would not recommend this law firm.

benchmark_result.review_text:
I was disappointed in the entire process. I was assigned to 3 different attorney's. Each time a little information and understanding of my case was lost. I feel my settlement amount suffered due to this issue. After 2 years, I received a final settlement offer. I was advised if I didn't accept it and was awarded benefits through the court process, the insurance company can stop paying benefits at any time or possibly not be awarded any benefits. Then the process of proving disability begins all over again... the thought of that vicious circle just wasn't worth it. The entire staff is really nicr,  professional & qualified.
I'm not sure if they have a large turnover issue resulting in my unfortunate situation. It would have been great if someone keep me in the loop. I kept calling & sending messages without replies. I finally I went through the main number and was told my attorney was no longer working there. I received a call after my case was reassigned to new attorney. He was well versed in my case and very positive. Then towards the end of the process, I received a call from the final attorney to tell me he was now handling my case. He was totally uninformed of the details of my case.

I think there could have been more communication from lawyer at times I had to ask what’s going on with the case.

themes.sentence_snippet: …how certain actions would affect me later. My case was also delayed nearly five months, only to end in a plea deal that…

benchmark_result.review_text:
I wasn’t planning to leave a review, but after today I’m done. I hired Hoglund & Mrozik, P.L.L.C. for a criminal matter. At first, everything seemed solid. Unfortunately, that didn’t last.

I was given guidance that ultimately cost me over $1,000 and wasn’t fully explained—especially how certain actions would affect me later. My case was also delayed nearly five months, only to end in a plea deal that likely could have happened much earlier.

I was prepared to let all of that go. What pushed me over the edge was being contacted six months after my case was settled and told I still owed money. Thankfully, I keep records. After I provided receipts, they reviewed it again and confirmed I had, in fact, paid in full.

Make of that what you will.

benchmark_result.review_text: Paid $2400 cash to retain Robert Hoglund plus $348 filing fee just to end up in bankruptcy court to pay the trustee another $2500. I didn’t do my homework and suffered in the end. I initially called for a consultation and before I knew it I was signing papers. The staff (paralegals) Erica Olson, Elvin Rengifo, and Abbi Kutz are rude, don’t return calls, and only want to respond through emails at their convenience. I emailed Robert Hoglund not long before my court date and said how I was going to leave a review and he ended up sending Doug Weimerskirich to represent me after he assured me that he’d be in court with me. The entire process was a huge headache that was costly. When you retain them, they work for you, you don’t work for them. I somehow spent a lot of time sending in bank statements, check stubs, and other documents that I could have done had I filed on my own. I DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS LAW FIRM. Call around and get free consultations before you pay this company. YOU’VE BEEN WARNED.

benchmark_result.review_text: If I could go after them for misrepresentation I definitely would. Shortly after becoming my attorney and having a court date there was a massive wait. I had to call the disability office in the cities and see what is going on because my attorney would always say "we are waiting for them to make a decision." What ended up happening is they were waiting for an income statement that wasn't coming for 6 months. I had to tell my attorney when our next court date was more than once because they wouldn't keep checking on my case. I also was on the phone with the judge's assistant trying to get things moving forward because my attorney was never checking on my case to try and move things forward. I was approved for disability but they stopped working when they got paid. I tried multiple times to speak to an attorney about my back pay but I was belittled by multiple attorneys saying "I should be grateful for them getting me approved." When I explained the situation they simply told me "everything looks and sounds correct." My back pay was incorrect and after a year of them getting phone calls from me asking for updates they said "nothing can be done and everything is correct as far as your back pay." I then called and asked to speak to the brand partner and left a message but I only received a letter from the brand partner saying "nothing can be done about your back pay" so I had to make a few calls. After one phone call I was able to get my back pay figured out and the disability office said that there was an error and they were going to correct it. If I didn't do the leg work that my attorney never did I wouldn't be receiving the correct amount because my attorney wasn't going to get paid anymore money because they already maxed out their pay. Stay away from Hoglund Law.

the firm canceled or didn’t show up for multiple calls
the communication barrier made it impossible to get the correct information across.

themes.sentence_snippet: …it feels like they’re not taking that seriously at all. The lack of communication, the failure to provide real legal cou…
…to help me. Instead, they’ve shown nothing but neglect and unprofessionalism. My family and I depend on these benefits…

benchmark_result.review_text:
Completely disappointed – do not trust this firm with your disability case.

I am beyond frustrated and disappointed with this law firm, and I feel compelled to warn others. Not only did they fail me during my appeal, but they also handled my original Social Security disability application, which was denied. Now I’m left in an even worse situation than before, struggling to get the help I need.

After my initial denial, I reached out to the firm for assistance with the appeal, hoping they’d offer the expertise I needed. Instead, the firm canceled or didn’t show up for multiple calls after I informed them about the denial. It was like they didn’t care, and I was just left hanging.

Eventually, a paralegal named Juliana was supposed to follow up with me, but instead I got an outsourced contractor from Mexico who was completely unprepared and unfamiliar with my case. I have cognitive impairments, and the communication barrier made it impossible to get the correct information across. Not only could she not understand what I was saying, but she couldn’t even spell basic details correctly.

By the end of the call, it became clear that this person wasn’t offering any real legal advice—she was just filling out an appeal form on the Social Security website. This person had access to all of my information, including my social security number, and was working from her home in a small town in Mexico, which raised data security concerns. The attorney I hired has not spoken to me. They never reviewed my case, never offered insight into why my initial application was denied, and didn’t suggest any ways to strengthen the appeal. Just today I contacted Carrie, my case manager, to ask questions about an appointment that was scheduled by social security and she was unable to answer what the tests listed on the appointment sheet were.

This firm handled my original disability application, and I trusted them to do everything they could to help me. Instead, they’ve shown nothing but neglect and unprofessionalism. My family and I depend on these benefits to survive, and it feels like they’re not taking that seriously at all. The lack of communication, the failure to provide real legal counsel, and the overall disregard for my case is infuriating.

I would strongly advise anyone seeking Social Security disability assistance to avoid this firm at all costs. Save yourself the stress, time, and heartache.

benchmark_result.review_text:
I wish I could say great things like other reviews. Genuinely I felt hopeful with the first call. I was treated well, brought in with open arms.

Sadly it just went down from there. In the year I was with them, I was the only one making the main calls to check up on my claim. My case manager barely explained things and told me "You were the one that came to us late". I did my best to keep my cool but I felt like I was just being treated as another number. I called the service quits, and despite my letter stating I was unhappy with the service, I'm still going to be charged if I ever get approved for my disability claim.

Surely other people will have better experiences, but this was mine.

benchmark_result.review_text:
I am a former client, regardless of their response. Schwebel Goetz & Sieben (SGS): be prepared, this process is about money, not justice or principle.

Over two years ago, tragedy struck our family. Our 20-year-old son died, and our wrongful death claim involved the bar he visited that night. In our first meeting with our SGS attorney, we stressed that our priority wasn’t money—it was justice. He said he understood, would file charges, and would be willing to go to court. Their investigator gathered evidence supporting our claim. Then months of near silence passed. Eventually, our attorney said he would file charges; he never did. He addressed me by my deceased son’s name, twice.

Over the following months, I personally uncovered two more critical pieces of evidence. That I had to do this myself was a major red flag—this was their job. With this new evidence, our attorney set up early mediation without our approval and before filing charges. Reluctantly, we agreed. Before the mediation, he oddly showed doubt about our claim, then shook my hand and said, “You’ve done more than my investigators.”

Mediation failed. The mediator, hand-picked by SGS, was arrogant and dismissive. Our attorney repeated that the next step was to file charges. Again, it didn’t happen. Later, the insurance company made another offer based on the mediator’s suggestion. We rejected it—too low and we still wanted charges filed. But our attorney insisted we accept, otherwise find a different attorney. He gave no specifics as to why, just vague comments about the evidence.

We never requested early mediation. We were pushed into accepting a settlement we didn’t believe in. Three times we were promised that charges would be filed—they never were. The bar avoided court, responsibility, and bad publicity. For these reasons, they won, not us.

Our attorney knew the opposing counsel, had worked with him before, and likely will again. This was a business negotiation, not representation. Once you hire an SGS lawyer, you give up control. Your goals become secondary to their financial incentives. We settled, but justice wasn’t served and our son deserved better.

I received my determination/settlement from Social Security and did not hear from them until a month later. I think they should tell you that no matter how long you wait for your disability you are not likely to get any more than 18 months of benefits. That info is buried in your paperwork from the SS office with your settlement. They did get it done! Glad it is over.

themes.sentence_snippet: …ked with Kong (not sure that is spelled right). He was very understanding and helped me through paperwork. Then, the den…

benchmark_result.review_text: I guess my experience with them may not be complete, but from what I have experienced so far I am frustrated. I went through them to apply for social security disability. I worked with Kong (not sure that is spelled right). He was very understanding and helped me through paperwork. Then, the denial came 3 months later, something he told me to expect. I called him. He said he would send my case to the appeals department and they would call me the next day or two. About 5 days later, I called them to see when the appeals was planning on calling. The person said they had tried to call me that morning—no message was left. I seldom answer a number I don't recognize and figure if it is important they will leave a message. The call representative said she would pass my message on to "her". It is 24 hours later and no call. It is very frustrating because there is a date on the letter and appeals have to be done within the time frame allowed. I would have given Kong a 5 star rating, but overall it is a 3. If there was a 2 1/2 I would have done that. I kind of wish I had gone to another lawyer. UPDATE: I have a new case manager now. She seems to be conscientious regarding my case. I seldom reach her on the first call, but she usually returns my call by the next day or day after.
